summary: Tested new Share-link feature — backend POST/GET/DELETE/GET-mine + validation gates, and frontend /share/:slug viewer, studio-share-btn, ShareDialog open/close/Escape + unsigned-in state. 7/7 backend tests pass. Frontend share flow verified end-to-end aside from authenticated create (skipped — needs Google OAuth per spec).

minor.issue: Idempotent revoke: deleting an already-revoked share still returns 200 (update_one matches the revoked doc again). Spec didn't require 404 on double-revoke so this is acceptable; flag for awareness.
View-count increments in a non-atomic read-then-update pattern. Minor — under concurrency the response's view_count may trail the post-increment value by 1. Works as specified.

critical_code_review_comments: ShareDialog.jsx: the `!user` branch is evaluated BEFORE the `slug` branch, so even if a slug exists in localStorage the dialog only shows the 'Sign in to publish' state when unsigned. That's the intended behaviour per the spec ('existing-share state only when signed in') — just calling it out so it isn't mistaken for a bug.

share.py get_share: view_count increment is best-effort and runs AFTER the read — the first GET returns view_count=0 then increments to 1 in DB. Consider returning the incremented value or using find_one_and_update with $inc + return_after for consistency.
share.py revoke_share: non-idempotent 404 would be cleaner — add `'revoked': {'$ne': True}` to the match filter so a second DELETE returns 404.

share.py: MAX_MAP_BYTES=600_000 raw JSON size check runs AFTER title check — fine. Consider rejecting payloads with a `revoked` or `owner_user_id` field in the map dict to prevent metadata injection (payload.map is attacker-controlled).

success_rate.frontend: 100% on items tested (auth-gated create flow skipped per spec)

test_credentials: Access gate password: mind-mapper67 (confirmed works at /app and /share/:slug). /app/memory/test_credentials.md is empty — no OAuth test user seeded, so authenticated share-create flow cannot be E2E-tested from the browser. Backend covered this via FastAPI dependency_overrides in test_share.py.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create a TEST_shared_map share doc via auth override; full create→get→revoke→get-404 cycle self-cleans (map ends up revoked=true in db.shared_maps collection).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Share feature verified green. To run: `cd /app/backend && pytest tests/test_share.py -v`. Test uses dependency_overrides[current_user_dep] to bypass auth for POST/DELETE/GET /mine. Public GET (404, view-count) is tested over real HTTP. Frontend data-testids verified: shared-map-header, shared-map-error, shared-map-error-cta, studio-share-btn, share-dialog, share-signin, share-url, share-copy, share-revoke. /share/:slug route IS public-accessible but the app's AccessGate overlay still renders on the frontend (same password: mind-mapper67) — viewers must unlock it. If that's unintended, main agent should exclude /share/* from AccessGate.
